title: Lastrea thelypteris
taxonomy: Lastrea thelypteris is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a part of the genus Lastrea, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Lastrea is a synonym for the genus Cyclosorus, which is a group of ferns known for their simple, lobed leaves. The family Thelypteridaceae is a group of ferns that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions, and are known for their simple, lobed leaves. Lastrea thelypteris is an important part of its ecosystem, providing food and shelter for various animals and insects.
uses: Lastrea thelypteris, commonly known as the lady fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain. In some cultures, the tea made from the leaves is believed to have a calming effect on the mind and body. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Lastrea thelypteris for medicinal purposes.
